senses.gloss: Lord; Saint; Mar (honorific title for a man of religion, most often a saint)

examples.text: لَيْسَ كُلُّ مَنْ يَقُولُ لِي: يَا مَار! يَا مَار! يَدْخُلُ مَلَكُوتَ السَّمَاوَاتِ، بَلْ مَنْ يَعْمَلُ بِإِرَادَةِ أَبِي الَّذِي فِي السَّمَاوَاتِ
examples.english: Not everyone who says to me, O Lord! O Lord! will enter the kingdom of heaven, but only the one who does the will of my Father who is in heaven.
the above-mentioned, the aforesaid, the above

senses.gloss: going by, walking past, riding past, going across, walking, transient
to budge, to move forth, to rise across the surface, to fluctuate, to undergo commotion

examples.text: ولا تحدث الأعاصير في العادة بشكل متزامن في المحيطين، فحين يمور المحيط الهادئ بالعاصفة يكون المحيط الأطلسي هادئا".
examples.english: Typhoons don’t usually appear simultaneously in the two oceans: When the Pacific Ocean is moved by a storm the Atlantic Ocean is calm.

etymology.text:
From Classical Syriac ܡܪܝ (mār(ī)), the first-person singular possessed form of ܡܪܐ (mārā, “lord, master”).

Derived from the active participle of مَرَّ (marra, “to pass, to stroll by”).

body.text: It moved from side to side,
like the knee-pan on the knee;
to and fro, like as the tall palm-tree moves;
The upper bones of his two arms move from side to side.
The spear-head moved from side to side in the person pierced
The thrust inclines to the right and left.
The dust moved to and fro:
it was in a state of commotion; in a state of tumult:
(S, * M, Msb, K:) said of the sea, (Msb,) &c.: (M:)
it was in a state of quick motion
(Msb.) It is said in a trad., that when the soul, or spirit, was blown into Adam,
It circulated, and moved to and fro, in his head, and he sneezed.
On the day when the heaven shall actually be in a state of commotion,
shall move from side to side:
so accord. to AO and Akh: (S:) or
(T.) And in a trad. of Ibn-Ez-Zubeyr,
With troops moving to and fro, in a state of commotion, like the leg of the locust,
by reason of their multitude. (TA.) You say also,
The she-camel was in a state of commotion, and reeled, in her pace,
and in like manner you say of a mare. (TA.) ―         -b3-
, a saying of the Arabs, related by IAar, (TA,)
I know not whether he have come to low country,
turned and returned to high country
have come to the low country,
upon the surface of the ground; (T, S, M, Msb, K;) and in like manner you say of tears, meaning they
upon the surface of the ground,
